name: Acebo y Las Fuentes Trail
name_original: Ruta del Camino del Área Recreativa del Acebo y Las Fuentes — ida y vuelta desde Navas de Estena
description: Explore the easy 4.7-mile Acebo y Las Fuentes Trail in Cabañeros National Park, featuring a boardwalk, diverse forest, and river views.
region_intro: Hiking around Navas De Estena offers access to the Cabañeros National Park, known for its diverse natural environment. The region features the Boquerón del Estena valley, celebrated for its distinctive flora, ancient geological strata, and fossils dating back over 400 million years. The Estena River is a central feature, with trails following its banks through riparian scenery where Mediterranean vegetation coexists with northern species. Impressive geological formations, such as the columnar quartzite "Estena Towers," add to the varied landscape, which also includes streams, micro-peat bogs, and Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and cork oaks. Best hiking trails around Navas De Estena The most popular hiking route is Boquerón del Estena Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 11 minutes to complete. This easy path allows exploration of distinctive flora and ancient geological formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of La Viñuela and of the Cañada Real – Parque Nacional de Cabañeros, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This route offers diverse ecosystems and views within Cabañeros National Park. Local hikers also love the Route of the Arroyo de Lanchas, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through riparian scenery and Mediterranean forests, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Hiking around Navas De Estena is defined by riverine landscapes, ancient geological formations, and varied Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Navas De Estena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Navas De Estena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Navas De Estena?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes in the Navas De Estena area, offering a range of difficulties from easy strolls to more challenging treks within the Cabañeros National Park.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking trails in Navas De Estena?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy trails suitable for families. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018237349/boqueron-del-estena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boquerón del Estena Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.4 km long and allows you to explore distinctive flora and ancient geological formations, including fossils, along the Estena River.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on a hike in Navas De Estena?
A: Hiking in Navas De Estena offers a diverse landscape. You'll encounter riverine scenery along the Estena River, impressive geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt;, and varied Mediterranean forests with holm oaks, cork oaks, and gall oaks. The area is also known for its unique flora, including yews and birches, and ancient geological strata with fossils dating back over 400 million years.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a longer experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087302914/route-of-la-vinuela-and-of-the-canada-real-parque-nacional-de-cabaneros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of La Viñuela and of the Cañada Real – Parque Nacional de Cabañeros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.9 km path that explores diverse ecosystems within the national park. While most routes are easy to moderate, the varied terrain offers opportunities for longer excursions.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Navas De Estena area?
A: Many of the trails in Navas De Estena, particularly those within Cabañeros National Park, are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019709607/route-of-the-arroyo-de-lanchas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Arroyo de Lanchas&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a trail that offers a loop through riparian scenery and Mediterranean forests.

Q: What are the highlights or points of interest along the trails?
A: Beyond the stunning natural landscapes, you can discover significant geological sites with ancient fossils, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5636470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giant Marine Worm Tracks&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt; are also a dramatic visual element. For those interested in water features, nearby waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3336126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Los Navalucillos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5633420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera Chica Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; can be explored.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Navas De Estena?
A: The trails in Navas De Estena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique natural environment, the well-preserved geological features, and the peaceful riverine landscapes.

Q: Is it possible to see wildlife while hiking in Navas De Estena?
A: Yes, the diverse habitats, including streams, micro-peat bogs, and Mediterranean forests, provide excellent opportunities for wildlife viewing. You might spot various flora and fauna, including aquatic birds and numerous Mediterranean species, especially along the Estena River.

Q: Are there any specific trails that feature waterfalls?
A: While the main hiking trails focus on the river and geological features, the wider region around Navas De Estena is home to several waterfalls. You can explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5634889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera de Horcajo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de la Gimena Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible from nearby routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navas De Estena?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for hiking during most of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Winter can also be a good time for quieter trails, while summer might be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Estena River?
A: The Estena River is a central feature of the region, and many trails follow its banks, offering beautiful riparian scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188091122/acebo-y-las-fuentes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acebo y Las Fuentes Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides an easy walk through areas influenced by the river's ecosystem.
tips: A small town in the province of Ciudad Real, at the door of the P.N. of Cabañeros. Nearby you will find two interesting hiking routes: Senda del Boquerón de Estena and, less well known, Route of the Camino del Área Recreativa del Acebo y Las Fuentes.

At the beginning of the route we find a Recreational Area of the Holly Table, the first section we go along a wooden walkway, accessible to people with reduced mobility. We will also find a viewpoint. The rest of the route is a dirt road where we will pass through a forest of holm oaks, gall oaks and rebollos.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Acebo y Las Fuentes Trail?
faqs.answer: The full route, starting from Navas de Estena, is approximately 7 km and typically takes about 3 hours to complete as a round trip. If you start from the recreational area, the hike is shorter, around 3 km, and can be finished in about 1 hour and 15 minutes. Some sources also mention a 7 km loop that takes roughly 1 hour and 30 minutes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied and picturesque scenery. The initial 450 meters feature a wooden boardwalk alongside the Estena River, making it accessible. Beyond that, the path widens and winds through a diverse forest of holm oaks, gall oaks, and Pyrenean oaks. You'll also encounter stony clearings known as "canchales" and a rich riparian ecosystem with ash trees, willows, and other plants.

faqs.question: Are there any notable natural features or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are several. A significant highlight is a viewpoint offering stunning vistas of the "Boquerón del Estena," a narrow gorge carved by the river. The trail also passes two traditional, non-chlorinated water sources, Fuente Fría and Fuente de las Chinas. Near one of these springs, you can observe a unique and fragile peat bog ecosystem.

faqs.question: Is the Acebo y Las Fuentes Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trail. However, as it is located within Cabañeros National Park, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Acebo y Las Fuentes Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly recommended. In spring, the vegetation is lush and wildflowers bloom, while autumn offers beautiful fall colors. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable, and winter can be pleasant on clear days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for Cabañeros National Park?
faqs.answer: There is no specific entrance fee or permit required to hike the Acebo y Las Fuentes Trail itself. However, as it is located within Cabañeros National Park, it's important to respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and taking all trash with you.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail can be accessed from Navas de Estena, where you can find parking. Alternatively, you can start directly from the recreational area, which shortens the hike significantly. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked near these starting points.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children or individuals with reduced mobility?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is considered easy and is very suitable for families. The initial 450-meter section features a wooden boardwalk alongside the Estena River, which is specifically designed to be accessible for individuals with reduced mobility, making it a great option for a wide range of visitors.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino del Acebo y Las Fuentes, Camino del Espinar, and Camino del Puerto de los Bidones, offering a glimpse into the wider network of trails in the area.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or restaurants near the trailhead in Navas de Estena?
faqs.answer: Yes, Navas de Estena is a small settlement and offers basic amenities. You can find local restaurants or places to grab a bite before or after your hike. It's a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail showcases a rich Mediterranean ecosystem. You'll walk through forests of holm oaks, gall oaks, and Pyrenean oaks. Along the river, look for ash trees, willows, rushes, brambles, and hawthorns. The rare peat bog ecosystem hosts unique plants adapted to nitrogen-poor soils. Keep an eye out for various bird species and other local wildlife typical of Cabañeros National Park.
